name: Open Telemark, Natural Turn to Outside Swivel and Feather Ending
preceding_figure_names: Closed Impetus and Feather Finish, Feather Step, Hover Cross, Hover Feather, Hover Telemark, Natural Hover Telemark, Natural Telemark, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Zig-Zag from PP
following_figure_names: Reverse Turn, Closed Telemark, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending, Three Step (at corner), Change Of Direction (at corner), Hover Telemark (at corner), Reverse Wave (at corner), Weave from PP (after step 7), Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending, Natural Zig-Zag from PP (after step 7)
notes:
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could be danced by checking OP on any Feather Movement taken DC and turning 1/4 to R. Follow --Silver--Feather Ending--Natural Turn (when danced as steps 4-6 above)--Natural Weave from PP--Gold--Curved Feather to Back Feather from PP--Natural Zig-Zag from PP. The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could also be danced by checking OP on any Feather taken DW without turn and followed as in the previous paragraph.
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could also be danced by checking OP on any Feather taken DW without turn and followed as in the previous paragraph.
When the Outside Swivel is danced at the side of the room to be followed by Feather Ending into Top Spin, 1/8 turn to R is made on step 7 and 8-10 will be taken DC against LOD. Weave from PP may also be danced following this underturned Outside Swivel, Man using the Basic Weave alignment. If dancing this underturned Outside Swivel at a corner steps 8-11 will be taken DC of new LOD. Follow as for Feather.
Follower details include Heel Turn execution, head turning discipline, shaping to left, tracking Man's movement, and maintaining continuous smooth motion without stopping. The Feather finish is achieved through the base with head returning to left.
When the Outside Swivel is danced at the side of the room to be followed by Feather Ending into Top Spin, 1/8 turn to R is made on step 7 and 8-10 will be taken DC against LOD. Weave from PP may also be danced following this underturned Outside Swivel, Man using the Basic Weave alignment. If dancing this underturned Outside Swivel at a corner steps 8-11 will be taken DC of new LOD. Follow as for Feather.
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could also be danced by checking OP on any Feather taken DW without turn and followed as in the previous paragraph.
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could also be danced by checking OP on any Feather taken DW without turn and followed as in the previous paragraph.

steps.action: RF fwd and across in PP and CBMP

comments: Compress right side, leading Lady into swivel action. Rotate through the hip, arms keep rotating across the body to right (arms are still in front of body, not behind.)

steps.action: LF back in CBMP, RF crosses in front without weight
steps.alignment: DW Toe turned in. End facing DC
comments: Keep head poised through diagonal. Diagonal shape is important, do not take head weight back.

steps.action: RF fwd and across in PP and CBMP
LF diag fwd preparing to step OP L side leading
LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP
LF fwd preparing to step OP L side leading
LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP
steps.alignment: Facing DC against LOD (moving to center)
comments: Allow Man to get ahead of you
Left shoulder connects to left hip. Keep shaping to left.
Allow arms to swing across the body.
Then dance straight forward, follow the Man with upper body, legs are tracking straight.

steps.action: RF to side and slightly back
